title: Optical apparatus and methods
abstract: There is described apparatus 12, 112 for using energy or power from first radiation 14 at one or more first wavelengths to amplify or generate second radiation 16 at one or more second wavelengths. The apparatus 12 for amplifying second radiation 16 comprises an optical resonator 18 comprising a solid-state Raman gain medium 20 such as diamond. The optical resonator 18 is configured such that the first radiation 14 and second radiation 16 propagate through the Raman gain medium 20 such that the first radiation 14 produces Stokes-shifted radiation which in turn amplifies the externally-generated second radiation 16 emitting at the one or more wavelengths corresponding to the second Stokes shift of the first radiation 14 in the Raman gain medium 20. The apparatus 112 for generating second radiation 116 uses a first optical resonator 118 to generate first radiation 114 for pumping an intracavity or extracavity second optical resonator 119, 219 comprising a Raman gain medium 120, 220 such as diamond to generate second radiation 116, 216.
claims: Claims 1 . Apparatus configured to use energy or power from first radiation at one or more first wavelengths to amplify externally-generated second radiation at one or more second wavelengths, the apparatus comprising: an optical resonator comprising a solid-state Raman gain medium such as diamond, the optical resonator being configured such that the first radiation and externally-generated second radiation propagate through the Raman gain medium such that the first radiation produces Stokes-shifted radiation which in turn amplifies the externally-generated second radiation at one or more wavelengths corresponding to a Stokes-shift within the Raman gain medium. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the optical resonator is configured in such a way that it does not produce any Raman oscillation at one or more Stokes-shifted wavelengths. 3. The apparatus of claim 1 or 2, wherein the first radiation is configured to generate radiation at one or more first Stokes-shifted wavelengths within the Raman gain medium, the radiation at the one or more first Stokes-shifted wavelengths being configured to resonate with the optical resonator and amplify radiation within the Raman gain medium at one or more second Stokes-shifted wavelengths that correspond to the one or more second wavelengths of the second radiation. 4. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the first radiation is configured to pump the Raman gain medium to generate the radiation at the one or more first Stokes-shifted wavelengths and wherein the radiation at the one or more first Stokes-shifted wavelengths is configured to pump the Raman gain medium to amplify the radiation at the one or more second Stokes-shifted wavelengths. 5. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the optical resonator is configured to: permit transmission of the first radiation into the optical resonator; and permit transmission of the second radiation into optical resonator such that the second radiation propagates at least once through the Raman gain medium before a portion of the amplified second radiation is transmitted out of the optical resonator. 6. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the apparatus is configured such that the second radiation propagates along a beam axis that is not collinear with a beam axis of the first radiation. 7. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the externally-generated second radiation is configured to seed amplification of the second radiation within the Raman gain medium via the effect of stimulated Raman scattering. 8. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the apparatus further comprises a seed laser such as a laser diode, the seed laser being configured to generate the second radiation externally of the optical resonator and direct the second radiation into the optical resonator for amplification thereof. 9. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the apparatus further comprises a pump laser such as a solid-state laser, fibre laser or laser diode, the pump laser being configured to generate the first radiation externally of the optical resonator and direct the first radiation into the optical resonator. 10. A remote sensing apparatus such as a Light Imaging, Detection, and Ranging (LIDAR) apparatus, gas detection apparatus or differential LIDAR (DIAL) apparatus comprising the apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 9. 1 1 . A method for using energy or power from first radiation at one or more first wavelengths to amplify externally-generated second radiation at one or more second wavelengths, the method comprising: propagating the first radiation and externally-generated second radiation into an optical resonator comprising a solid-state Raman gain medium such that the first radiation amplifies the externally-generated second radiation at one or more wavelengths corresponding to a Stokes shift within the Raman gain medium. 12. The method of claim 1 1 , comprising using the amplified second radiation in a remote sensing application such as Light Imaging, Detection, and Ranging (LIDAR), gas detection or differential LIDAR (DIAL). 13. Apparatus configurable to produce laser radiation in different modes, the apparatus comprising: a first optical resonator comprising a laser gain medium, the first optical resonator being configured to generate first radiation in a first mode, the first optical resonator comprising or being coupled to a second optical resonator, the second optical resonator comprising a solid-state Raman gain medium such as diamond, the first and second optical resonators being configured such that the first radiation resonates within the first optical resonator and propagates through the Raman gain medium in order to generate Stokes-shifted radiation; a third optical resonator comprising the laser gain medium, the third optical resonator being configured to generate second radiation in a second mode; and a mode selection element configurable to operate the apparatus in one of the first and second modes. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the first radiation is configured to generate radiation at one or more first Stokes-shifted wavelengths within the Raman gain medium, the radiation at the one or more first Stokes-shifted wavelengths being configured to resonate with the second optical resonator and produce radiation within the Raman gain medium at one or more second Stokes-shifted wavelengths that correspond to the one or more second wavelengths. 15. The apparatus of claim 13 or 14, wherein the second optical resonator comprises a first optical coupler and a second optical coupler, wherein in a first mode, the first optical coupler is configured to: permit transmission of the first radiation into the second optical resonator; and reflect radiation at the one or more first Stokes-shifted wavelengths within the second optical resonator, and wherein the second optical coupler is configured to reflect radiation at the one or more first Stokes-shifted wavelengths within the second optical resonator, and optionally wherein the second optical coupler is configured to: reflect the first radiation such that the first radiation resonates within the first optical resonator; or permit the first radiation to be transmitted therethrough such that the first radiation is transmitted out of the second optical resonator via the second optical coupler. 16. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the first and second optical couplers are configured to reflect the second radiation and wherein the second optical coupler is further configured to permit transmission of a portion of the second radiation out of the second optical resonator. 17. The apparatus of claim 15 or 16, wherein the first optical resonator comprises the second optical coupler and a third optical coupler configured to reflect the first radiation, the first optical coupler being positioned optically between the second and third optical couplers. 18. The apparatus of claim 15 or 16, wherein the first optical resonator comprises a fifth optical coupler and a third optical coupler configured to reflect the first radiation, the apparatus further comprising a focusing element provided between the fifth optical coupler and the first optical coupler to focus the first radiation into the Raman gain medium. 19. The apparatus of claim 17 or 18, wherein the apparatus further comprises a fourth optical coupler which, when used in conjunction with the third optical coupler, defines the third optical resonator, wherein in the second mode, at least one of the third and fourth optical couplers is configured to transmit a portion of radiation that resonates within the third optical resonator out of the third optical resonator. 20. The apparatus of any one of claims 13 to 19, wherein the apparatus is configured such that the Stokes-shifted radiation is generated along a beam axis that is collinear with a beam axis of the first radiation. 21 . The apparatus of any one of claims 13 to 20, wherein the mode selection element comprises a polarisation selection element configured to select a radiation polarisation that resonates in the first or third optical resonators such that in the first mode the radiation resonating within the first optical resonator has a first polarisation and in the second mode the radiation resonating within the third optical resonator has a second polarisation that is different to the first polarisation. 22. The apparatus of any one of claims 13 to 21 , wherein the second optical resonator is one of: intracavity of and extracavity to the first optical resonator. 23. A ranging or target illumination apparatus, a target designation apparatus or a remote sensing apparatus such as a Light Imaging, Detection, and Ranging (LIDAR) apparatus, apparatus for measuring distance with radiation or an apparatus for illumination of objects for their designation comprising the apparatus of any one of claims 13 to 22. 24. A method for producing laser radiation in different modes, the method comprising: providing a first optical resonator operative to generate first radiation in a first mode, the first optical resonator comprising or being coupled to a second optical resonator, the second optical resonator comprising a solid-state Raman gain medium such as diamond, the first and second optical resonators being configured such that in operation, the first radiation resonates within the first optical resonator and propagates through the Raman gain medium in order to generate Stokes-shifted radiation; providing a third optical resonator comprising the laser gain medium, the third optical resonator being operative to generate second radiation in a second mode; and selecting, with a mode selection element, a mode of operation to generate either the first or second radiation in the first or second mode, respectively. 25. The method of claim 24, comprising using at least one of: the first radiation and the second radiation in a ranging or target illumination application, a target designation application or a remote sensing application such as a Light Imaging, Detection, and Ranging (LIDAR), measuring distance with radiation or illuminating objects for their designation.
